entry.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
entry.content:
Stolen OAuth tokens, which are at the root of these breaches, "are the new attack surface, the new lateral movement," a researcher notes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
entry.content:
The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender Into Attacker Tool
entry.content:
Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
entry.content:
A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Electricity Is a Growing Area of Cyber Risk
entry.content:
IT has long been concerned about ensuring systems receive the right amount of electricity. Cyberattackers are realizing they can manipulate voltage fluctuations for their purposes, too.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
entry.content:
Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Africa Relinquishes Cyberattack Lead to Latin America — For Now
entry.content:
The volume of cyberattacks targeting Africa declined in the past year, with weekly attacks down 22%, as attackers seemingly shifted their focus to other regions.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'Zealot' Shows What AI's Capable of in Staged Cloud Attack
entry.content:
The proof of concept revealed AI-based attacks unfold too fast for human defenders to respond, and that AI evinced more autonomous behavior than expected.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: China-Backed Hackers Are Industrializing Botnets
entry.content:
China's state-backed groups are now using covert networks of compromised devices to execute attacks in a low-cost, low-risk, and deniable way.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: AI Phishing Is No. 1 With a Bullet for Cyberattackers
entry.content:
In the past six months, companies have seen a significant influx of AI-powered phishing, as cyberattackers progress from small campaigns to 1-to-1 personalized attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Copperhelm Raises $7 Million for Agentic Cloud Security Platform
entry.content:
The Israel-based company, which just emerged from stealth mode, was founded by cloud and security experts from RSA, McAfee, and Unity. The post Copperhelm Raises $7 Million for Agentic Cloud Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Bitwarden NPM Package Hit in Supply Chain Attack
entry.content:
Tied to a fresh Checkmarx supply chain attack claimed by TeamPCP, the incident references the Shai-Hulud worm. The post Bitwarden NPM Package Hit in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Trump Administration Vows Crackdown on Chinese Companies ‘Exploiting’ AI Models Made in US
entry.content:
The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies’ exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models. The post Trump Administration Vows Crackdown on Chinese Companies ‘Exploiting’ AI Models Made in US appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Locked Shields 2026: 41 Nations Strengthen Cyber Resilience in World’s Biggest Exercise
entry.content:
Locked Shields has grown significantly over the past 16 years, with only four nations participating in the first edition. The post Locked Shields 2026: 41 Nations Strengthen Cyber Resilience in World’s Biggest Exercise appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Why Cybersecurity Must Rethink Defense in the Age of Autonomous Agents
entry.content:
From autonomous code generation to decision-making systems that initiate actions without human intervention, the industry is entering a new phase. The post Why Cybersecurity Must Rethink Defense in the Age of Autonomous Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: In Other News: Unauthorized Mythos Access, Plankey CISA Nomination Ends, New Display Security Device
entry.content:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Supreme Court hacker sentenced, Lovable exposed user data, Google expands enterprise security. The post In Other News: Unauthorized Mythos Access, Plankey CISA Nomination Ends, New Display Security Device appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Pre-Stuxnet Sabotage Malware ‘Fast16’ Linked to US-Iran Cyber Tensions
entry.content:
It targeted high-precision calculation software to tamper with results and packed a self-propagation mechanism. The post Pre-Stuxnet Sabotage Malware ‘Fast16’ Linked to US-Iran Cyber Tensions appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ADT says customer data stolen in cyber intrusion
entry.content:
The home security company ADT said cybercriminals breached company systems on Monday and stole a “limited set” of customer and prospective customer information.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran’s cyber threat may be less ‘shock and awe’ than ‘low and slow,’ officials say
entry.content:
Officials and experts believe the most likely threat from Iranian hackers is not a digital shock-and-awe campaign, but something quieter: opportunistic intrusions, dressed up to look bigger than they are.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
entry.content:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
entry.content:
An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
entry.content:
Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Conducting U.S. Ransomware Attacks
entry.content:
The third ransomware negotiator indicted for his role in conducting BlackCat ransomware attacks on U.S. companies in 2023 has entered […] The post Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Conducting U.S. Ransomware Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Ransomware Attack on Hospital Caribbean Medical Center Affects 92,000 Individuals
entry.content:
A ransomware attack on Hospital Caribbean Medical Center in Puerto Rico has affected up to 92,000 individuals. Data breaches have […] The post Ransomware Attack on Hospital Caribbean Medical Center Affects 92,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Minidoka Memorial Hospital Recovering from Easter Cyberattack
entry.content:
Minidoka Memorial Hospital was the victim of a cyberattack on Easter morning, and two further healthcare providers have confirmed they […] The post Minidoka Memorial Hospital Recovering from Easter Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: OPM’s Plan to Collect Federal Employees’ Health Insurance Data Attracts Strong Criticism
entry.content:
A proposal to allow the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to collect the personally identifiable health information of federal employees […] The post OPM’s Plan to Collect Federal Employees’ Health Insurance Data Attracts Strong Criticism appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: FREE Webinar Next Week: 2025 HIPAA Breaches & Fines: What Went Wrong and Your 2026 Action Plan
entry.content:
In 2025, hundreds of healthcare data breaches exposed tens of millions of patient records — and the OCR enforcement record […] The post FREE Webinar Next Week: 2025 HIPAA Breaches & Fines: What Went Wrong and Your 2026 Action Plan appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Absolute Dental Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit for $3.3M
entry.content:
A class action lawsuit filed against Absolute Dental Group, LLC, and Judge Consulting, Inc., over a 2025 data breach has […] The post Absolute Dental Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit for $3.3M appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Healthcare AI Firm Sued Over Alleged Unlawful Disclosures of Genetic Data
entry.content:
Tempus AI, a publicly traded healthcare artificial intelligence company, is facing multiple class action lawsuits over the alleged unauthorized collection […] The post Healthcare AI Firm Sued Over Alleged Unlawful Disclosures of Genetic Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.content:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) has announced four financial penalties to […] The post OCR Fines Four Regulated Entities for HIPAA Violations That Led to Ransomware Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Alabama Ophthalmology Associates Data Breach Settlement Gets First Nod
entry.content:
Alabama Ophthalmology Associates, P.C., has settled a class action lawsuit that was filed in response to a January 2025 cyberattack […] The post Alabama Ophthalmology Associates Data Breach Settlement Gets First Nod appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: House Republicans Make New Attempt to Introduce Federal Data Privacy Legislation
entry.content:
House Republicans have made a fresh attempt to introduce federal data privacy legislation that, if passed, will replace the current […] The post House Republicans Make New Attempt to Introduce Federal Data Privacy Legislation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: BlueLeaks 2.0: 7,300+ Schools, Referral Systems Reported, and a Breach Navigate360 Still Hasn’t Publicly Confirmed
entry.content:
Overview and Background This is the first of what will likely be several updates to this site’s exclusive reporting on the “BlueLeaks 2.0” incident that exposed anonymous and sensitive tips by and about students on a platform that promised them anonymity and security. DDoSecrets.org named the incident “Blue Leaks 2.0” because, like a previous leak... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Outside FDA, Inside the Crosshairs: Cybersecurity Risks for General Wellness and Fitness Products
entry.content:
Troutman Pepper Locke writes: In Part One of this series, we discussed how wellness products sit at the intersection of Food and Drug Administration (FDA), Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA), Federal Trade Commission (FTC), and state privacy/breach laws. In Part Two, we analyzed FDA’s 2026 General Wellness guidance and what it means for device-level cybersecurity expectations.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: NOT for Sale! BlueLeaks 2.0 Hacktivist decides not to sell dataset with sensitive data
entry.content:
Just when I thought I might be done with work for the day, DataBreaches received an email from “Internet Yiff Machine” (IYM), the hacktivist responsible for hacking P3 Global Intel in what has been called the “Blue Leaks 2.0” breach. As most readers know by now, IYM provided a dataset of 8.3 million tips that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Mile Bluff Medical Center says security incident that involved data encryption disrupted phone, computer systems
entry.content:
DysruptionHub reports: Mile Bluff Medical Center in Mauston, Wisconsin, said Tuesday that a security event involving data encryption disrupted some phone and computer functions, prompting clinical teams to shift to downtime procedures while patient care continued. The hospital said it activated security protocols immediately after detecting the issue and began an investigation with internal experts... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: RAMP Uncovered: Anatomy of Russia’s Ransomware Marketplace
entry.content:
Pierluigi Paganini reports: A leaked database from RAMP gives us a rare look behind the curtain. It shows how cybercrime works when it becomes structured, commercial, and repeatable. Instead of random hackers acting alone, RAMP functioned like a business platform where criminals could sell access, recruit affiliates, advertise ransomware, and negotiate deals in private. What... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Half a million Britons’ medical data were offered for sale on Alibaba in major UK Biobank breach
entry.content:
Harry Taylor reports: Data from 500,000 people who volunteered their health information to the UK Biobank has been breached and offered for sale online in China. Technology Minister Ian Murray said that information of all half a million members had been listed for sale on the website Alibaba, as he called the breach an “unacceptable abuse” of data. He... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Trigona Affiliates Deploy Custom Exfiltration Tool to Streamline Data Theft
entry.content:
From the so-there-they-are! dept Out of sight, out of mind? It seems like ages ago that DataBreaches last reported on the Trigona ransomware group, but it was actually in September 2023. After that, DataBreaches lost track of them, and after a few months, concluded that they had disappeared or disbanded. But a check of ransomlook.io... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Healthcare AI Firm Sued Over Alleged Unlawful Disclosures of Genetic Data
entry.content:
Steve Alder reports: Tempus AI, a publicly traded healthcare artificial intelligence company, is facing multiple class action lawsuits over the alleged unauthorized collection and disclosure of genetic testing results, which were derived from genetic testing by Ambry Genetics Corporation (Ambry Genetics). Tempus AI used Ambry Genetics’ genetic database to train its AI models. Tempus AI... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: South Korea’s regulator fines matchmaking service Duo $830,000 over data breach
entry.content:
Hyun Su-a reports: Duo Info, South Korea’s top matchmaking company, leaked the personal information of 430,000 members, authorities said. The leaked items went far beyond names and email addresses to include religion, hobbies, height, weight, education and remarriage history. Excluding income and asset information, virtually all of the members’ personal details were exposed externally. The... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: OCR Announces Settlements of Four Ransomware Investigations that Affected Over 427,000 Individuals
entry.content:
Yesterday, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), Office for Civil Rights (OCR) announced settlements with four regulated entities following separate ransomware investigations under HIPAA’S Security Rule. For those keeping count: the resolutions announced mark 19 completed investigations from ransomware breaches and 13 completed investigations in OCR’s Risk Analysis Initiative. The settlements follow... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.content:
Verification alert Listings attributed to AKIRA have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: AKIRA | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

entry.title: Cosmetics giant Rituals discloses data breach affecting customers
entry.content:
Dutch cosmetics giant Rituals disclosed a data breach after attackers stole the personal information of an undisclosed number of customers from its "My Rituals" membership database. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New Checkmarx supply-chain breach affects KICS analysis tool
entry.content:
Hackers have compromised Docker images, VSCode and Open VSX extensions for the Checkmarx KICS analysis tool to harvest sensitive data from developer environments. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Trigona ransomware attacks use custom exfiltration tool to steal data
entry.content:
Recently observed Trigona ransomware attacks are using a custom, command-line tool to steal data from compromised environments faster and more efficiently. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Bitwarden CLI npm package compromised to steal developer credentials
entry.content:
The Bitwarden CLI was briefly compromised after attackers uploaded a malicious @bitwarden/cli package to npm containing a credential-stealing payload capable of spreading to other projects. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Hackers exploit file upload bug in Breeze Cache WordPress plugin
entry.content:
Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the Breeze Cache plugin for WordPress that allows uploading arbitrary files on the server without authentication. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Over 10,000 Zimbra servers vulnerable to ongoing XSS attacks
entry.content:
Over 10,000 Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) instances exposed online are vulnerable to ongoing attacks exploiting a cross-site scripting (XSS) security flaw. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New ‘Pack2TheRoot’ flaw gives hackers root Linux access
entry.content:
A new vulnerability dubbed Pack2TheRoot could be exploited in the PackageKit daemon to allow local Linux users to install or remove system packages and gain root permissions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: New BlackFile extortion group linked to surge of vishing attacks
entry.content:
A new financially motivated hacking group tracked as BlackFile has been linked to a wave of data theft and extortion attacks against retail and hospitality organizations since February 2026. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: ADT confirms data breach after ShinyHunters leak threat
entry.content:
Home security giant ADT has confirmed a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
